charts.description: Cell differentiation: niche signals drive a master transcription-factor network that resolves a bistable lineage decision, with chromatin remodeling and cross-antagonism locking in a stable differentiated state.

sources.authors: Takahashi, K.; Yamanaka, S.

charts.description: Circadian clock: a transcription-translation feedback loop in which CLOCK-BMAL1 drives Per/Cry expression, and accumulating PER/CRY repress their own activators, generating a self-sustaining ~24 h oscillation.

sources.title: Transcriptional architecture of the mammalian circadian clock
sources.authors: Takahashi, J. S.

sources.title: Circadian rhythms from multiple oscillators: lessons from diverse organisms
sources.authors: Bell-Pedersen, D.; Cassone, V. M.; Earnest, D. J.; Golden, S. S.; Hardin, P. E.; Thomas, T. L.; Zoran, M. J.

sources.authors: Partch, C. L.; Green, C. B.; Takahashi, J. S.

nodes.label: Activate Per/Cry (E-box)
nodes.detail: Drives transcription of Period and Cryptochrome

nodes.label: PER/CRY accumulate
nodes.detail: Repressor proteins build up in cytoplasm

nodes.label: Repressor threshold?
nodes.detail: Has repressor reached the level for turnover?

charts.description: Epithelial-mesenchymal transition: TGF-beta/Wnt signals induce Snail/Zeb/Twist factors that repress E-cadherin, dissolve junctions and polarity, and confer a migratory mesenchymal phenotype that can revert via MET.

sources.authors: Lamouille, S.; Xu, J.; Derynck, R.

charts.description: Morphogen gradient patterning: a localized source secretes a diffusible morphogen whose concentration gradient is read by cells against thresholds to switch on distinct target genes, with feedback shaping the gradient.

sources.title: Positional information and the spatial pattern of cellular differentiation
sources.authors: Wolpert, L.

sources.title: Morphogen gradients: from generation to interpretation
sources.authors: Rogers, K. W.; Schier, A. F.
sources.journal: Annual Review of Cell and Developmental Biology

sources.title: Morphogen rules: design principles of gradient-mediated embryo patterning
sources.authors: Briscoe, J.; Small, S.

charts.description: Auxin signaling: rising auxin promotes TIR1/AFB-mediated ubiquitination and degradation of Aux/IAA repressors, freeing ARF transcription factors to drive auxin-response genes, with negative feedback from re-synthesized repressors.

sources.title: Auxin Signaling
sources.authors: Leyser, O.

sources.title: Mechanisms of auxin signaling
sources.authors: Lavy, M.; Estelle, M.

sources.title: Transcriptional Responses to the Auxin Hormone
sources.authors: Weijers, D.; Wagner, D.

nodes.label: Auxin binds TIR1/AFB
nodes.detail: Auxin acts as molecular glue for the SCF-TIR1 receptor

nodes.label: Auxin now low?
nodes.detail: Has auxin dropped below the response level?

charts.description: Central dogma: genetic information flows from DNA to RNA by transcription, mRNA is processed and exported, and ribosomes translate codons into a polypeptide that folds into a functional protein.

sources.title: Central dogma of molecular biology
sources.authors: Crick, F.

sources.title: Molecular Biology of the Cell (From DNA to RNA / RNA to Protein)

charts.description: CRISPR-Cas9: a guide-RNA-programmed Cas9 nuclease locates a PAM, checks guide-target complementarity, and cleaves both DNA strands, creating a double-strand break repaired by NHEJ or HDR to edit the locus.

sources.title: A programmable dual-RNA-guided DNA endonuclease in adaptive bacterial immunity
sources.authors: Jinek, M.; Chylinski, K.; Fonfara, I.; Hauer, M.; Doudna, J. A.; Charpentier, E.

charts.description: DNA replication: helicase unwinds the origin, primase/polymerase synthesize the continuous leading strand and discontinuous Okazaki fragments on the lagging strand, which are matured and ligated as the fork advances.

sources.authors: O'Donnell, M.; Langston, L.; Stillman, B.

nodes.label: Lagging: Okazaki fragments

nodes.label: Ligase seals nicks
nodes.detail: Fragments joined into a continuous strand

charts.description: Feedback inhibition: the end product of a biosynthetic pathway binds an allosteric site on the first committed enzyme (e.g., ATCase), shifting it to a low-activity state and reducing flux when product is abundant.

nodes.label: End product abundant?
nodes.detail: Is product above the set point?

charts.description: Homologous recombination repair: a double-strand break is resected to 3' ssDNA, coated by RPA then RAD51, which searches for a homologous template and invades it, with resolution via double-Holliday-junction or SDSA for accurate repair.

sources.title: Mechanism of eukaryotic homologous recombination
sources.authors: San Filippo, J.; Sung, P.; Klein, H.

sources.title: Regulation of homologous recombination in eukaryotes
sources.authors: Heyer, W. D.; Ehmsen, K. T.; Liu, J.

sources.authors: Wright, W. D.; Shah, S. S.; Heyer, W. D.

nodes.label: RAD51 filament (BRCA2)
nodes.detail: RAD51 replaces RPA to form the presynaptic filament

nodes.label: D-loop; DNA synthesis
nodes.detail: Extension primed from the invading strand

charts.description: mRNA splicing: the spliceosome assembles on a pre-mRNA (U1 at the 5' site, U2 at the branch point, then the tri-snRNP), catalyzes two transesterifications through a lariat intermediate, and joins exons, iterating over each intron.

sources.title: The spliceosome: design principles of a dynamic RNP machine
sources.authors: Wahl, M. C.; Will, C. L.; Lührmann, R.

sources.title: Spliceosome structure and function
sources.authors: Will, C. L.; Lührmann, R.

sources.title: A day in the life of the spliceosome
sources.authors: Matera, A. G.; Wang, Z.

charts.description: Nucleotide excision repair: bulky DNA lesions are recognized by global-genome (XPC) or transcription-coupled sensing, TFIIH opens the helix, the damage is verified, dual incision excises a short oligonucleotide, and the gap is filled and ligated.

sources.title: Understanding nucleotide excision repair and its roles in cancer and ageing
sources.authors: Marteijn, J. A.; Lans, H.; Vermeulen, W.; Hoeijmakers, J. H.

sources.authors: Gillet, L. C.; Schärer, O. D.

nodes.label: Stalled RNA pol signals
nodes.detail: TC-NER triggered at lesions blocking transcription

nodes.label: TFIIH unwinds lesion
nodes.detail: Helicases open DNA around the damage

charts.description: TPP riboswitch: an aptamer in the mRNA leader senses thiamine pyrophosphate; ligand binding refolds the expression platform into a terminator/sequestering structure that switches the downstream gene off, while the ligand-free form keeps it on.

sources.title: Thiamine derivatives bind messenger RNAs directly to regulate bacterial gene expression
sources.authors: Winkler, W.; Nahvi, A.; Breaker, R. R.

sources.authors: Serganov, A.; Nudler, E.

nodes.label: Nascent mRNA w/ aptamer
nodes.detail: Leader RNA bearing a TPP aptamer

charts.description: RNA interference: Dicer cleaves long double-stranded RNA into siRNAs that load into Argonaute/RISC; the passenger strand is discarded and the guide strand directs Ago2-catalyzed slicing of complementary mRNA, silencing the gene.

sources.title: Potent and specific genetic interference by double-stranded RNA in Caenorhabditis elegans

nodes.label: Dicer cleaves to siRNA
nodes.detail: Dicer processes dsRNA into short duplexes

charts.description: Eukaryotic transcription initiation: TFIID/TBP recognizes the core promoter, general factors recruit RNA polymerase II to form the pre-initiation complex, TFIIH melts the DNA, and CTD phosphorylation drives promoter escape into elongation.

sources.authors: Sainsbury, S.; Bernecky, C.; Cramer, P.

sources.authors: Haberle, V.; Stark, A.

sources.title: The role of general initiation factors in transcription by RNA polymerase II
sources.authors: Roeder, R. G.

nodes.label: CTD phosphorylation
nodes.detail: CDK7 phosphorylates the CTD, releasing Pol II

charts.description: Eukaryotic translation initiation: eIF4F binds the 5' cap, the 43S pre-initiation complex scans the 5' UTR for an AUG start codon, recognition triggers eIF2 GTP hydrolysis, and 60S joining forms an elongation-competent 80S ribosome.

sources.title: The mechanism of eukaryotic translation initiation and principles of its regulation
sources.authors: Jackson, R. J.; Hellen, C. U.; Pestova, T. V.

nodes.label: Scan 5' UTR
nodes.detail: Ribosome scans toward the start codon

charts.description: Trp operon control: high tryptophan lets the Trp repressor block the operator, while at low tryptophan transcription proceeds and attenuation via leader-peptide ribosome stalling decides whether RNA polymerase reads through to the structural genes.

sources.title: Attenuation in the control of expression of bacterial operons
sources.authors: Yanofsky, C.

sources.title: Transcription attenuation: once viewed as a novel regulatory strategy, it has been found in many forms
sources.authors: Yanofsky, C.

sources.title: Transcription attenuation: a highly conserved regulatory strategy used by bacteria
